Company description:



ATTURRA OPERATIONS PTY LTD



Job description:



What you'll be doing

At Atturra, as a Senior Integration Consultant, you'll be working on some of the highest profile transformation and technology projects in the Asia Pacific region.

The depth, breadth and scope of our projects means you are continually developing your skillset and the value you can deliver.

These are projects that are making a real difference in people's lives, allowing you to build the future, together.

Key Responsibilities

Contribute to technical design discussions.
Lead or contribute to code reviews and technical design/specifications.
Lead or contribute to development of custom code.
Contribute to the enhancement of in-house delivery methodologies.
Contribute to the review and analysis of business requirements.
Perform technical assessments and sizing.
Contribute to the delivery process by being part of client meetings, making presentations, and at times working as part of the tender response team.

Working Conditions

Full time permanent opportunity
Based in Sydney
AU Citizen a MUST

What will make you succesful?

The position necessitates a highly motivated individual who can provide strong technical skills, along with leadership, guidance, and coaching abilities, to deliver quality focused outcomes.

At least 10 years of experience in a similar role or capacity.
Experience linking cross-functional applications withwith Boomi platform.
Knowledge of API Management and API Gateway Technology (such as Apigee, Axway, Azure, AWS, etc.)
Experience in integrating with core enterprise software applications (such as Salesforce, Oracle, SAP, NetSuite, MS Dynamics 365, Workday, Sage etc.), both on-premises and cloud.
Experience with business and technical requirement analysis, business process modelling/mapping, methodology development, and data mapping.
Strong understanding of enterprise application integration patterns.
Expertise in the assessment, architectural design, and implementation of integration patterns including middleware, bulk data integration, real-time data integrations, file-based integrations, cloud integrations, process integrations, and event processing.
Expertise with B2B integration concepts, standards, architectures (AS2, X12, EDIFACT, XML).
Knowledge of relational database design and data management techniques.

You will also have:

Excellent communication skills & the ability to consult with stakeholders at all levels.
Demonstrated ability to contribute and self-manage within a small team environment.
Ability to build rapport within the workplace, work well within a team, excellent networking skills and the ability to perform in a fast-paced environment.
Business acumen and ability to identify client business drivers.

Why is this an exciting opportunity?

Atturra is one of the fastest growing ASX-listed advisory and IT solutions companies in Australia, giving you a strong foundation to fulfil your career ambitions.

We currently have 1100+ professionals working across Australia, New Zealand, Singapore and Hong Kong who are using innovation to lead the way.

The Data & Integration business unit helps clients unlock the full potential of data by integrating systems and applications, enabling digital transformation through our comprehensive on-premises, hybrid, and cloud integration solutions. We specialise in Boomi, Smartsheet, and OpenText.

What our people say:

"Atturra has a great working environment and management are very supportive of their staff. We celebrate success and have fun while working hard."

Why you'll love working here

Culture of possibility

At Atturra we embrace innovation within our teams, fostering an environment of possibility and growth. We believe in creating a supportive and welcoming culture, where everyone is encouraged to achieve success through collaboration, hard work and dedication.

A strong set of company values

Our culture is reflected by our employees who demonstrate camaraderie, innovation, dedication, respect and excellence in everything they do. These traits have become integral to the way we do business and are the values we strive for.

Valuable employee benefits

Career growth via succession planning, internal mobility programs, and mentorship opportunities.
Ongoing investment in professional development through industry certifications.
Employee benefits, recognition, and wellbeing platform.Mental health support through our Employee Assistance Program.
Support for family and caring responsibilities, including paid parental leave.
Employee referral program, with monetary incentives offered.

Commitment to supporting Australia's veterans

Atturra is proud to be recognised as a Veteran Friendly Employer by the Department of Veterans Affairs' Veteran Employment Commitment (VEC) and is committed to supporting Australia's veteran community. We aim to enhance the employment experience for veterans by improving recruitment, support, retention, and leadership opportunities to ensure they have a meaningful and impactful career with us.

Recruitment Agencies/Headhunters:

We got this covered. Our Talent Acquisition team is working on our hiring demands and will reach out should we need extra support. Atturra only accepts referrals from agencies on our official supplier list via Agency Portal.

Agencies are required to go through our procurement process, have an approved fee agreement, and be invited to participate in hiring for a job opening by the Atturra Talent Acquisition team. Any profiles or referrals submitted outside of this process will be deemed invalid. We request that you do not contact Atturra employees outside of the Talent Acquisition team for any hiring-related queries. To become an official supplier, send an expression of interest via talentacquisition@atturra.com.

Atturra is passionate about playing an active role in addressing the digital skills gap and being an advocate for diversity and Inclusion, and welcomes applications from all irrespective of their gender, LGBTQI identity, ethnicity, religion, physical ability, or age.

We are seeking a highly capable, energetic and focussed Senior Business Planning Officer to work with our Canberra-based client.

One year into the delivery of an ambitious 10-year business strategy, they're now turning strategy into action — and they need an experienced planning professional to help make it happen.

The Opportunity

This is a hands-on, high-visibility contract role sitting within the Strategy and Finance team, reporting directly to the Business Strategy and Policy Lead. You'll be at the centre of a business-wide planning initiative, working with each of the organisation's six business groups to workshop, shape and document their individual business plans for 2026–27.

This is not a passive support role. You'll bring structure, clarity and quality to a fast-moving planning cycle — and leave behind tools and templates the organisation will use long after the contract ends.

Up to $95/hr (super inclusive)

6-month labour hire contract, with potential 6-month extension

Full-time or part-time considered (3–4 days per week for the right candidate)

Hybrid working — minimum 2 days per week at the Mitchell, Canberra office

Start date: on or before 1 May 2026

The Role

Working closely with the Business Strategy and Policy Lead, your key responsibilities will include:

Supporting the end-to-end planning, facilitation and documentation of business planning workshops across six business groups

Providing high-quality scribing during workshops and synthesising outputs into clear, well-structured planning documents

Developing practical MS Word and PowerPoint templates and tools for business-wide use, enabling teams to self-serve through the planning process

Translating strategy into actionable business plans that clearly link group activities to the organisation's 10-year strategic direction

Reviewing and improving existing SharePoint content for clarity, usability and accessibility

Developing materials that define strategy pathways, sequencing and dependencies

Managing multiple deliverables and competing priorities across a structured but fast-paced planning calendar

Please note: You must be available to attend a series of mandatory face-to-face workshops in Canberra:

Monday 11 May | Wednesday 13 May | Wednesday 20 May | Thursday 21 May | Wednesday 27 May 2026

The Ideal Person

You have at least 3 years' experience in business planning, strategy support or a similar officer-level role within a medium to large organisation. You're comfortable in rooms with senior stakeholders, you write and structure documents with precision, and you know how to turn a messy workshop conversation into something polished and usable.

You will also bring:

Proven experience supporting and documenting business planning or strategy workshops, including high-quality scribing and synthesis of outputs

Strong skills in MS Word and PowerPoint, including the ability to develop clean, professional templates for business use

Demonstrated ability to develop planning materials that translate strategy into practical, actionable documentation

Strong written and verbal communication skills, with experience preparing content for senior and executive audiences

The ability to manage competing priorities and deliver quality outputs under tight deadlines

Tertiary qualifications in business, public policy or a related discipline

Full working rights in Australia and willingness to undergo a police check

How to apply

Click on ‘Apply' to submit your resume & cover letter.

This role is based in Canberra, so you must be either willing to relocate, or already be a permanent resident of the ACT.

We encourage a gender diverse working environment and welcome applications that reflect this.
